value_chain_position: Credit-scoring analytics and decisioning-software platform occupying the market-data-and-analytics layer of fintech rails — the FICO score is a near-mandatory toll on virtually every US consumer-credit origination, consumed by the bureaus (EFX/TRU/EXPN) below it and by lenders, GSEs, rating agencies, MBS investors, insurers and prudential regulators downstream. FICO is now attempting to move UP the chain via its Mortgage Direct License Program, disintermediating the three nationwide bureaus.
gm_trajectory: EXPANDING and the cleanest moat lie-detector in the screener: gross margin ~78% (FY23) to ~80% (FY24) to 82.2% (FY25) on +13% 3-yr revenue CAGR; non-GAAP operating margin expanded ~450bps YoY to 58% (Q2'25) — pricing power, not commoditization.
bull_thesis: FICO is a near-irreplaceable toll-taker on US consumer credit with the cleanest pricing-power signature in the universe: gross margin EXPANDING to 82.2% on +16% revenue, ROIC 41%, FCF margin ~39%, Piotroski 8/9. The Scores moat is structural — the FICO score is embedded in GSE standards, bank underwriting policy, MBS ratings and regulatory capital frameworks; management cites ~mid-to-high-90s share with 'known predictable performance through the Great Recession' (Q4'25). Growth is royalty PRICE, not volume: mortgage-origination revenue +48/53/60% YoY across Q2'25-Q1'26 on flat volumes, with a fourth royalty hike taking the 2026 mortgage score to ~$10 plus a new $33 per-closed-loan performance fee designed to 'capture downstream IP value' (rating agencies, MBS investors, insurers) FICO historically gave away for free. The Direct License Program is a genuine strategic counter — Xactus, Cotality, Ascend, CIC, MeridianLink signed; resellers representing ~90% of mortgage volume engaged — letting FICO disintermediate the bureaus and own distribution. Software adds a second engine: platform ARR +17-37%, platform NRR 110%, ACV bookings accelerating $16.8M->$26.7M, Gartner/Forrester decision-intelligence leader, AWS strategic agreement, FICO-FFM foundation model and Plaid/UltraFICO cash-flow score. Guidance is a credibly-validated sandbag (beat 6/7 qtrs, 'you'd probably still think we were being too conservative,' Q1'26). For the bull, GSE dual-adoption of FICO 10T (2026-04-22) is actually a tailwind — 10T is the more predictive model and ~$1.6T eligible servicing has signed up. Conditions: mortgage price power holds despite VantageScore, FICO 10T captures the conforming model migration, and platform compounds into the $822M base.
bear_thesis: The whole 2026 growth engine rests on extracting MORE price from a US-mortgage channel that just lost its monopoly. On 2026-04-22 FHFA/HUD blessed VantageScore 4.0 ALONGSIDE FICO 10T for Fannie/Freddie/FHA loans, and VantageScore 4.0 is usable immediately for GSE conventional loans at roughly $0.99/score versus FICO's ~$10 — a ~10x price gap inside FICO's crown-jewel conforming channel. Mortgage-origination revenue is ~42-44% of Scores revenue and the dominant growth driver, and it is PRICE on flat-to-declining volume; if FICO holds price it risks ceding conforming share, if it defends share it surrenders the pricing that drives the model. The aggressive 2X royalty hike has already drawn a Senator Hawley referral to the FTC over mortgage-score pricing — monetizing hardest exactly as the political spotlight turns on. Management spent three calls dismissing this exact risk ('the market share we're not very worried about, to be frank,' Q3'25) — a credibility tell. The balance sheet is NOT a fortress: NET DEBT $3.44B and NEGATIVE equity (ROE -110.6%) from buyback-funded leverage, with Q1'26 repurchases at ~$1,707 — well above today's ~$1,179, i.e. capital burned near the top. Software segment growth is only +3% despite 'momentum' framing; non-platform legacy is in structural decline (-2% to -13%). Open-market insiders: 0 buys / 4 sells into a 20% drawdown. At ~29-37x forward earnings — a steep premium to bureau peers at ~12-20x P/E — the stock prices continued disruptor success; any conforming-share erosion or price rollback compresses both the numerator and the multiple. Confirmation: VantageScore conforming-share data through 2026-2027, FTC action on pricing, or a mortgage-volume air-pocket if rates stay high.
sop_bull: Favorable EBIT-multiple SoP (segment EBIT x premium multiples; SUBTRACT $3.44B net debt; ~24.4M shares). Scores EBIT ~$1.05B (90% margin on $1.17B rev) at 28x = $29.5B; Software EBIT ~$0.23B (28% margin on $822M rev) at 22x = $5.0B. Gross EV ~$34.5B, less $3.44B net debt = ~$31.1B equity => ~$1,275/share (~40x TTM EPS $31.57). Bull assumes mortgage price power holds, 10T wins the conforming migration, and platform re-rates.
sop_bear: Adverse SoP ASSUMING THE THEME PAUSES 12-18 MONTHS (VantageScore takes conforming share + 2026 price hike partially rolls back under FTC pressure; mortgage-volume air-pocket). Scores EBIT $1.05B de-rates to 16x = $16.8B; Software EBIT $0.23B at 12x = $2.8B. Gross EV ~$19.6B, less $3.44B net debt = ~$16.2B equity => ~$663/share (~21x TTM EPS). This is the price the market migrates toward if the disruptor pricing story stalls.
sop_breakdown: Segment SoP on EBIT multiples, net debt subtracted. Scores: EBIT ~$1.05B (90% op margin on $1.17B FY25 rev) x 22x = ~$23.1B — the high-multiple toll-taker, but discounted from the 28x bull case for live VantageScore/FHFA share risk and FTC pricing overhang. Software/Applications: EBIT ~$0.23B (28% margin on $822M rev) x 16x = ~$3.7B — a 16x for a +3% segment carried by a fast-growing platform sub-line. Gross EV ~$26.8B. LESS net debt $3.44B (a liability, NOT net cash) = ~$23.4B equity / 24.4M sh = ~$959/share. Cross-check: equals ~30x TTM EPS $31.57, a justified premium to bureau peers (EFX ~20x, EXPN ~20x P/E) for FICO's higher margins and standard-setter position, but BELOW the current ~$1,179 / ~37x.
risk_reward: Unfavorable from here. Downside-to-bear ~$663 = ~-44% from $1,179; upside-to-base ~$960 = ~-19% (i.e. the base SoP is BELOW the current price); upside-to-bull ~$1,275 = ~+8%. Risk/reward ~ -44% downside vs +8% bull-only upside = roughly 5.5:1 AGAINST. The market already prices the disruptor outcome; the margin of safety the screener flags (64%, fair value $1,936 on Buffett model) is not corroborated by a segment SoP that nets to ~$960 after the $3.44B debt and the new competitive regime.
catalyst_status: FIRED — the load-bearing GSE event already happened: FHFA/HUD adopted VantageScore 4.0 AND FICO 10T for Fannie/Freddie/FHA on 2026-04-22 (VantageScore live immediately for GSE conventional; FICO 10T historicals Summer 2026). The 2026 royalty hike to ~$10 is also live and has drawn a Hawley/FTC pricing probe. Per debate rules catalyst_status is recorded but does NOT drive the verdict — which turns on theme durability, load-bearing position, GM trajectory and reinvestment economics.
peer_comps_note: theme_map true_competitors = EQUIFAX, TRU, EXPN.L, plus VEN/NRDS/OPEN/AFRM. FICO sits ABOVE the bureaus in the chain (it consumes their data; they distribute its score) and earns ~82% gross / ~58% op margin vs bureau ~30-40% op margins, so it warrants a premium — but bureau peers trade ~12.7-20x EV/EBITDA and ~20x P/E (EFX ~16-20x EV/EBITDA, EXPN ~12.7x), while FICO trades ~29x forward / ~37x TTM P/E. The premium is partly earned (margins, standard-setter), partly the very disruptor-pricing story now contested by VantageScore. A ~22x-EBIT Scores multiple in the base case keeps FICO above the bureaus without underwriting the full pre-competition premium.
consensus_delta: The street narrative has actually MOVED toward our view, which removes the arbitrage. Pre-2026 consensus underwrote FICO as an uncontested monopoly compounding price indefinitely (implied targets ~$1,971). Since the 2026-04-22 GSE dual-adoption the street has re-cut: targets trimmed to ~$1,728 (median ~$1,872), UBS Neutral at $1,350, and the stock is down ~20% from its 2/28/2026 high. So the consensus is no longer 'looking the wrong way' — it has already begun pricing the VantageScore threat. What remains arguably still-too-optimistic: (1) consensus forward EPS growth +26.1% assumes the 2026 ~2x royalty hike sticks through an active FTC/Hawley probe; (2) it assumes negligible conforming-share loss to a ~$0.99 GSE-blessed competitor; (3) it carries FICO at a ~29x premium to bureau peers at ~20x as if the monopoly were intact. Our SoP (~$960 base, net of $3.44B debt) says even the de-rated street is paying up. There is no positive expectations gap left to exploit — the easy disruptor re-rate is spent, and the residual risk is to the downside.
valley_of_death: Next 3-9 months carry several live hazards that hit BEFORE any vindication: (1) VantageScore 4.0 is usable NOW for GSE conventional loans — the first conforming-share and pricing data points land through 2026 and any visible FICO share leakage or a competitive price response would compress both estimates and the multiple; (2) the Hawley referral / potential FTC action on mortgage-score pricing is an open political-headline risk into a US election-adjacent year, and any pricing remedy directly hits the 42-44%-of-Scores mortgage engine; (3) mortgage VOLUME is rate-dependent and management has explicitly built 'extra conservative' haircuts and 'a pretty big assumption for reduction because of trigger leads' (Q4'25) — if rates stay high, the volume leg of mortgage Scores softens just as price is contested; (4) software CCS usage headwinds ('customers chose to delay or downsize… due to macro volatility,' Q2'25) and lumpy point-in-time/PS revenue make near-term software prints choppy. None of these is a cash-burn or maturity crisis — FCF is ~$893M TTM and Altman-Z 10.6 — but each is a re-rating risk while the stock sits at a premium multiple.
positioning_washout: No dividend-cut or pivot-driven mechanical liquidation dynamic here — FICO pays no meaningful dividend, so there is no yield-focused base to be forced out. The relevant washout is multiple-compression-driven: FICO is a crowded quality-growth/'compounder' holding owned by growth and quality funds at a ~29-37x multiple premised on uncontested pricing. If conforming-share or FTC-pricing data confirms the moat is contested, the de-rating from a 'monopoly multiple' to a 'contested-premium multiple' (our base ~30x vs current ~37x, and bear ~21x) is the washout — and momentum/quality factor models would mechanically trim as the price-vs-200d (already -18%) and analyst-target velocity (-13%) deteriorate further. The 0-buy / 4-sell open-market insider posture into the drawdown gives no support.
forcing_function: The hard dated catalysts now cut AGAINST a fresh long: (1) Summer 2026 — Enterprises publish historical FICO 10T scores and begin the conforming model migration, the first real read on 10T-vs-VantageScore conforming adoption; (2) the next 2-3 FICO earnings calls (next ~Apr/Jul 2026) — first quarters carrying actual VantageScore competition and the live 2026 pricing, where management's 'we're not worried' will be tested against B2B mortgage-Scores growth and any pricing commentary; (3) any FTC/Hawley action or a negotiated pricing concession. Each is a date on which the residual monopoly premium can be challenged; none is a positive forcing function that re-rates the stock UP from here.
moderator_conclusion: FICO is a genuinely elite business and the moat lie-detector passes cleanly — gross margin EXPANDING to 82.2% on +16% revenue, ROIC 41%, ~39% FCF margin, a structurally load-bearing position (load_bearing_score 5) embedded in GSE, bank, MBS-rating and regulatory frameworks. Reinvestment economics are superb and the Direct License Program is a credible attempt to move up the chain and own distribution. So as a QUALITY franchise this is an A-tier asset. But this committee allocates on theme durability AND price, and on both the current setup is unattractive. The load-bearing theme fact — the US conforming-mortgage price/royalty monopoly — was directly contested on 2026-04-22 when FHFA/HUD blessed VantageScore 4.0 (live now, ~$0.99) alongside FICO 10T, and FICO's aggressive ~2x 2026 royalty hike drew a Hawley/FTC probe. The growth engine is PRICE on a ~42-44%-of-Scores mortgage base, which is exactly the leg now exposed. The street has already begun pricing this (targets cut ~$1,971->~$1,728, stock -20%), so the expectations arbitrage is gone. My segment SoP — Scores EBIT ~$1.05B at 22x + Software EBIT ~$0.23B at 16x, LESS $3.44B net debt (a liability, not the screener's phantom net cash) over ~24.4M shares — nets to ~$960/share, BELOW the ~$1,179 price; the bull SoP (~$1,275) requires the full pre-competition premium to survive, and the bear (~$663) is the path if the theme pauses 12-18 months. Risk/reward is ~5:1 against (-44% to bear vs +8% to bull). The screener's 64% margin-of-safety is an artifact of the Buffett/IV models and the mislabeled net-cash field, not corroborated by parts. VERDICT C / conviction 2: a superb company, but the disruptor re-rate is FIRED, the moat's most valuable corner is freshly contested, and the price already pays for the optimistic outcome. value_conviction is also 2 — judged on valuation-vs-SoP and forensic quality alone (ignoring catalyst and regime), the stock trades above a debt-adjusted segment SoP, so even the pure value case is unattractive despite top-decile forensic quality. Watchlist for a real washout toward the bear SoP (~$660-800) before the quality justifies the entry.
